content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

February 27, 1987

Mr. James L. Hagar
Hagar & Cotten
Attorneys at Law
235 Jefferson Street
Vista, CA 92083

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A 87-033

Dear Mr. Hagar:

You have requested advice concerning the campaign reporting
provisions of the Political Reform Act. (Government Code Sections
81000-91015.)

FACTS

You are currently an elected member of the Vista Unified
School District Board. You will not be up for re-election for
four years. Your controlled committee ended 1986 with a zero
cash-on-hand figure, and the committee will only become active
again if you choose to run for re-election.

QUESTION

What are your reporting requirements and the reporting
requirements of your controlled committee during the period of
time you are not seeking re-election?

ANSWER

As an elected officeholder with a controlled committee, you
are required to file a Form 490, "Candidate and Officeholder
Campaign Statement-Long Form and Consolidated Campaign Statement,"
semi-annually whether or not you have received any contributions
or made any expenditures during the six-month period covered by
the statement. The period covered by a semi-annual statement is
January 1 through June 30 and July 1 through December 31 with
filing deadlines of July 31 and January 31 respectively.
(Government Code Section 84200.)

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

James Hagar
Page 2

     I have enclosed for your use and information, the 1987
"Information Manual on Campaign Disclosure Provisions of the
Political Reform Act" and the 1987 Form 490.

     If you have any additional questions concerning this matter,
please contact me at (916) 322-5662.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Diane M. Griffiths
                                        General Counsel

                                 By:    Mary Ann Kvasager
                                        Political Reform Consultant

DG:MAK:kt

Enclosures

HAGAR & COTTEN
ATTORNEYS AT LAW
235 JEFFERSON STREET
P.O. BOX 640
VISTA, CALIFORNIA 92083-0213
*TELEPHONE (619) 725-9882

JAMES L. HAGAR
CARY L. COTTEN

JAN 26 9 23 AM '87
January 22, 1987


Technical Assistance Analysis Department
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA  95804

Dear Sir or Madam:

I recently ran for re-election to the Board of Trustees
of the Vista Unified School District.  During that campaign,
I utilized a controlled committee--The Jim Hagar for School Board
Committee, I.D. Number 811447.  At this point, the campaign is
over, I have filed a Form 490 Campaign Statement covering the
period through December 31, 1986 with the San Diego County
Registrar of Voters.  Prior to December 31, 1986, I donated all
of my remaining campaign funds to the Vista Unified School District, so that I ended the year with $0 on hand.

To turn to the point of this letter, is there anything else
that I have to do at this point?  My committee will be dormant
for four years and will then be activated only if I choose to
run again.  Do I have to do anything now in regards to that
committee?

In advance, thank you for your assistance.

Very truly yours,

[signature]
James L. Hagar

JLH/nf
Enclosures

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission


January 27, 1987


James L. Hagar
Hagar & Cotten
P.O. Box 640
Vista, CA 92083-0213

Re: 87-033

Dear Mr. Hagar:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform
Act was received on January 26, 1987 by the Fair Political
Practices Commission. If you have any questions about your
advice request, you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions,
or more information is needed, you should expect a response
within 21 working days. You also should be aware that your
letter and our response are public records which may be
disclosed to the public upon receipt of a proper request for
disclosure.

Very truly yours,

Jeanne Pritchard
Chief
Technical Assistance and Analysis
Division

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660
